dict.string: DataMirror shows you what location data your iPhone exposes when Location Always is granted — including how apps can track your movements even in the background.
DataMirror shows your current coordinates, altitude, and accuracy level so you can see exactly what apps read when Location is permitted.
DataMirror demonstrates what the Camera permission unlocks for apps. No photos or video are ever taken or saved.
DataMirror shows what audio access looks like when the Microphone permission is granted. No audio is recorded or stored.
DataMirror illustrates what apps can see when Contacts access is granted. Your contact data is read locally and never leaves your device.
DataMirror shows what calendar data is accessible when this permission is granted. No events are read, modified, or stored.
DataMirror illustrates what Reminders access allows apps to do. No reminder content is read or modified.
DataMirror demonstrates what Full Photo Library access exposes. No photos are accessed, uploaded, or stored by this app.
DataMirror shows what Bluetooth permission enables — such as nearby device discovery. No connections are made.
DataMirror reads accelerometer and gyroscope data to show you the live motion signals your phone constantly generates.
DataMirror illustrates what Health access exposes when granted. No health data is read, stored, or shared.
DataMirror shows what Speech Recognition access allows apps to do. No audio is captured or transcribed by this app.
DataMirror uses Face ID to demonstrate the biometric authentication permission flow. No sensitive data is unlocked.
DataMirror shows you the impact of granting App Tracking Transparency. Allowing tracking here lets you see your advertising identifier — no data is shared with advertisers.
